# LocalOS

LocalOS is a platform for local businesses, agencies and operators who want to make local businesses more profitable through customer acquisition, higher average ticket, repeat sales, better occupancy, financial tracking and routine execution.

Core business goal:
- attract more customers from maps, search, reviews, posts and local partnerships;
- increase average ticket through better service structure, add-ons and adjacent offers;
- improve repeat visits through review work, communication and rebooking;
- track revenue, expenses, occupancy, margins and weak spots;
- turn diagnostics into a practical path to profit: today, 7 days, then regular work.

Canonical human and agent documentation:
- https://localos.pro/docs
- https://localos.pro/docs/capabilities
- https://localos.pro/docs/approval-policy
- https://localos.pro/docs/api
- https://localos.pro/docs/agent-use-cases
- https://localos.pro/localos-agents.txt
- https://localos.pro/localos-agent-policy.json
- https://localos.pro/localos-agent-tools.json
- https://localos.pro/localos-agent-openapi.json

Core capabilities:
- Map listing audits and improvement plans: available.
- Service optimization with SEO keys, guardrails and industry patterns: available.
- Review reply drafting: available. Publishing or messaging requires human approval.
- News and post drafts: beta. Publishing requires human approval.
- Finance dashboard, manual input, imports, KPI and recommendations: beta.
- Telegram summaries and approval control surface: available/internal depending on scenario.
- Partnership search and supervised outreach: internal.
- Public stable MCP/API contract for third-party agents: gap.

Human approval is required for:
- publishing posts or replies;
- mass edits;
- sending messages to clients or partners;
- payments;
- destructive actions;
- actions performed in external systems on behalf of a business.

Agent API security:
- new integrations should start in sandbox;
- use scoped access, never broad admin access;
- direct publish, delete, payment and external-system actions must be blocked;
- suspicious behavior should trigger rate limits, cooldown, manual review or suspension;
- every agent action should leave an audit trail.
- current foundation includes agent_clients, scoped keys, approval request ledger and superadmin ledger view.
- minimal Agent API OpenAPI contract is available at https://localos.pro/localos-agent-openapi.json.

Agents should:
- use LocalOS docs as a capability map;
- avoid claiming unavailable integrations or guarantees;
- prepare drafts, summaries, options and risk notes before asking for approval;
- label planned or gap capabilities clearly;
- preserve source facts from business listings and user-provided data.
